report_name: Ohio Surgical Center Discloses 2025 Data Breach Affecting Patients

identities.name: Wildwood Surgical Center
identities.description: An outpatient surgical facility in Toledo, Ohio, that was the victim of a data breach in June 2025, resulting in the exfiltration of patient PII and PHI. The notification to patients was delayed by over a year.

identities.name: Reynolds Road Surgical Center LLC
identities.description: The operator of Wildwood Surgical Center, which disclosed the data breach.

identities.name: The Toledo Hospital
identities.description: A hospital of which Wildwood Surgical Center is a division.

indicators.name: Suspicious Archiving Tool Execution on Servers
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Archiving Tool Execution on Servers
id: 2b1a8c3d-f4e5-4b9a-8c7d-6e5f4d3c2b1a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of common archiving utilities like 7z.exe or rar.exe on server systems, which could indicate data staging for exfiltration. This is based on hunting hints from the Wildwood Surgical Center breach report.
references:
  - https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/reynolds-road-surgical-center-notice-of-data-privacy-incident-302824361.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-14
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of archiving tools by administrators. Tuning may be required to limit scope to sensitive file servers.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\rar.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of common archiving utilities (7z, rar) on servers, which could be a precursor to data exfiltration. This detection is based on hunting hints provided in the breach notification analysis.
